ksa_text: Skill in designing human oversight mechanisms for AI systems, including designing human-machine interfaces for effective oversight, specifying human-in-the-loop protocols and escalation triggers, and assigning and validating competency of oversight personnel.

dcwf.top_element_label: Skill in conducting audits or reviews of technical systems.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
Skill in conducting audits or reviews of technical systems.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: AI Governance is a primary or secondary domain for the Chief Data Scientist. This skill KSA is within scope for the role's expected competency profile and seniority level (c suite).

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope (AI Governance), seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with inclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Pass 1 signal is moderate (composite 0.583). External reviewers may question whether this specific skill KSA is sufficiently distinctive for the Chief Data Scientist versus adjacent roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: NIST AI RMF, EU AI Act (Article 9), and OECD AI Principles confirm AI governance as a critical competency for responsible AI leadership.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: moderate framework signal is consistent with AI Governance appearing in job postings and role definition literature for Chief Data Scientist.

decision_rationale: AI Governance skill KSA is within scope for the Chief Data Scientist at the c suite tier. Seniority fit is appropriate. All three evaluation passes support inclusion.
